response.justification: Bogdan has valuable geospatial domain expertise (GISCollective, FOSS4G talks) and a demonstrated ability to quickly acquire new technical skills across languages and domains. However, the role's primary requirement of modern C++ development poses a significant concern—his last meaningful C++ work was ~10 years ago (2014-2015), and his career trajectory over the past 5 years has shifted toward TypeScript/web systems. While his software craftsmanship ethos and Linux experience are strengths, the lack of recent C++ proficiency combined with no explicit routing/navigation or distributed-systems depth makes him a borderline candidate who would require substantial ramp-up time.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain expertise through GISCollective (9+ years) and FOSS4G community engagement—highly relevant for routing tile optimization
Proven ability to learn and master new technical domains across web, mobile, embedded, and systems programming—aligns with the role's requirement to expand expertise
Strong commitment to software quality, testing, and craftsmanship (created testing libraries, TDD advocate, code kata organizer)
concerns: C++ skills are significantly outdated; last relevant C++ work was in 2014-2015, and modern C++ (C++17+) proficiency is unclear—critical gap for a role requiring modern C++ as primary skill

Career trajectory over the past 5 years (TypeScript, Node.js, web/product work) has moved away from systems programming, performance-critical code, and distributed systems work required for routing pipelines

No demonstrated experience with routing algorithms, navigation guidance systems, or the specific distributed-systems concepts (fault tolerance, consistency, service discovery) needed for data pipeline architecture

key_factors.factor: C++ recency and modern systems programming experience
